details.reason: Pending troponin requires follow-up result before discharge; rule out ACS in context of HF (I50.9) and hypertension (I10)
Pending troponin requires follow-up result before discharge; rule out ACS in context of HF (I50.9) and hypertension (I10)

params.value: 77-year-old female with active heart failure (I50.9), hypertension (I10), and polypharmacy (Z79.899). Troponin pending — ACS not yet ruled out. Repeat troponin ordered (order-9ce00a2cb0b5). Patient should not be discharged until troponin result is finalized and clinical picture is reassessed. Readmission risk is elevated; all active problems must be addressed and documented prior to discharge.

report.value: 77-year-old female with active heart failure (I50.9), hypertension (I10), and polypharmacy (Z79.899). Troponin pending — ACS not yet ruled out. Repeat troponin ordered (order-9ce00a2cb0b5). Patient should not be discharged until troponin result is finalized and clinical picture is reassessed. params.plan_text:
DISCHARGE PLAN — patient-v2-readmission-bgpm-000 DISCHARGE HELD pending troponin result (order-9ce00a2cb0b5). Patient must NOT be discharged until troponin is resulted and ACS is ruled out. ACTIVE PROBLEMS (all must be addressed before discharge):
1. I50.9 — Heart Failure: Optimize volume status; confirm euvolemia. Ensure patient and caregiver education on daily weights, fluid restriction, and HF action plan.
2. I10 — Hypertension: BP 137/82 on current regimen. Reconcile antihypertensive medications at discharge.
3. Z79.899 — Long-term polypharmacy: Full medication reconciliation required. Review for drug-drug interactions, renal dosing (Cr 1.1), and adherence barriers. PENDING RESULTS:
- Troponin (repeat): Must be reviewed and documented before discharge clearance. FOLLOW-UP PLAN (upon safe discharge):
- Cardiology or primary care follow-up within 7 days of discharge.
- Repeat BMP (renal function, electrolytes) within 1–2 weeks.
- Pharmacy medication reconciliation review. RISK COMMUNICATION:
- Elevated readmission risk documented (quality report quality-9bc466dfb649).
- All unresolved clinical risks (pending troponin, HF, polypharmacy) are explicitly documented and must not be suppressed from the discharge summary.
- Discharge summary must reflect all active diagnoses and pending results per chart-citation standards for accurate coding and billing.
